input.prompt:
My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.content:

and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

My MSTest project has a .testsettings file that configures test deployment, timeout,
and a code coverage data collector. After upgrading MSTest packages, the settings
seem to be ignored. How do I migrate this configuration?

data.reasoningText: The user has an MSTest project with a `.testsettings` file and wants to migrate it to the modern `.runsettings` format. They mention the settings are being ignored after upgrading MSTest packages. Let me look at their `.testsettings` file to understand what needs to be migrated, and I'll also invoke the relevant skill for MSTest migration guidance.

data.content: `.testsettings` files are **not supported** by modern MSTest (v2+). You need to migrate to a `.runsettings` file. Here's the equivalent for your configuration:

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<RunSettings>
  <RunConfiguration>
    <!-- Equivalent of TestTimeout property (30 seconds) -->
    <TestSessionTimeout>30000</TestSessionTimeout>
  </RunConfiguration>

  <MSTest>
    <!-- Per-test timeout in milliseconds -->
    <TestTimeout>30000</TestTimeout>
    <!-- Assembly resolution is handled automatically in modern MSTest -->
  </MSTest>

  <DataCollectionRunSettings>
    <DataCollectors>
      <DataCollector friendlyName="Code Coverage"
                     uri="datacollector://Microsoft/CodeCoverage/2.0"
                     assemblyQualifiedName="Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector.CoverageCollectorDataCollector, Microsoft.VisualStudio.TraceCollector, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=b03f5f7f11d50a3a">
        <Configuration>
          <CodeCoverage>
            <ModulePaths>
              <Exclude>
                <ModulePath>.*tests.*\.dll$</ModulePath>
              </Exclude>
            </ModulePaths>
          </CodeCoverage>
        </Configuration>
      </DataCollector>
    </DataCollectors>
  </DataCollectionRunSettings>
</RunSettings>

data.content:
I've created a `local.runsettings` file that replaces your `.testsettings`. Here's what changed:

| `.testsettings` setting | `.runsettings` equivalent |
|---|---|
| `TestTimeout` property (30s) | `<MSTest><TestTimeout>` + `<TestSessionTimeout>` |
| Assembly resolution | No longer needed — handled automatically |
| Code Coverage data collector | Updated to v2.0 URI under `<DataCollectionRunSettings>` |

**To use it**, run:
```
dotnet test --settings local.runsettings
```

Or reference it in your `.csproj`:
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <RunSettingsFilePath>$(MSBuildProjectDirectory)\local.runsettings</RunSettingsFilePath>
</PropertyGroup>
```

You can safely delete `local.testsettings` — it's completely ignored by modern MSTest/VSTest.
